side_effects: Senecio descoingsii is a plant species known to contain toxic compounds, specifically pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PAs). These compounds can cause liver damage and other health issues in humans if ingested. Ingestion of the plant can lead to symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Long-term exposure to these toxins can result in more severe liver damage and potentially life-threatening conditions. intro: Senecio descoingsii, also known as the Descoings ragwort or the Madagascar ragwort, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Senecio descoingsii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Senecio descoingsii include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
